explicit_text: I grew up in Marinette, Wisconsin, but when I turned 17, I got tired of my hometown and decided to leave. It was a small place, and I wanted to see more of the world, so I headed to Norfolk, Virginia to join my brother. Marinette was where my journey as a sailor began, but I was eager to move on to bigger ports and ships.
implicit_text: I grew up in a small town on the border of Wisconsin and Michigan, but when I turned 17, I got tired of my hometown and decided to leave. It was a small place, and I wanted to see more of the world, so I headed to a major naval city in Virginia to join my brother. That small hometown was where my journey as a sailor began, but I was eager to move on to bigger ports and ships.

explicit_text: Norfolk, Virginia was where I joined my brother Clifford, who was already a salt water sailor. We both got berths on the coal barge Saxola there, and it was from Norfolk that we started our voyage in February 1917. Norfolk was my first real step into the maritime world beyond my hometown.
implicit_text: That major naval city in Virginia was where I joined my brother Clifford, who was already a salt water sailor. We both got berths on a coal barge there, and it was from this port that we started our voyage in February 1917. This city was my first real step into the maritime world beyond my hometown.

explicit_text: The Saxola was a coal barge I sailed on with my brother in early 1917. We left Norfolk towing two other barges bound for Philadelphia with coal. During the trip, outside the Delaware Breakwater, we faced a fierce gale that caused one of the barges to sink, but thanks to my brother cutting the hawser, the Saxola was saved from being pulled down.
implicit_text: The coal barge I sailed on with my brother in early 1917 left the Virginia port towing two other barges bound for a large northeastern city with coal. During the trip, outside a well-known breakwater on the East Coast, we faced a fierce gale that caused one of the barges to sink, but thanks to my brother cutting the hawser, our barge was saved from being pulled down.

explicit_text: Philadelphia was our destination when we left Norfolk on the Saxola in February 1917. We were carrying coal there, but the journey was rough due to a sudden gale outside the Delaware Breakwater. Philadelphia marked an important port in my early sailing career.
implicit_text: Our destination when we left the Virginia port on the coal barge in February 1917 was a major city known for its industrial and shipping importance. We were carrying coal there, but the journey was rough due to a sudden gale outside a famous breakwater. That city marked an important port in my early sailing career.

explicit_text: While sailing outside the Delaware Breakwater in 1917, our barges were caught in a sudden gale with winds around 75 miles per hour. The conditions were so severe that the sails froze, and one barge got fouled on the anchor chain and sank. It was a dangerous spot that tested our seamanship early on.
implicit_text: While sailing outside a famous breakwater on the East Coast in 1917, our barges were caught in a sudden gale with winds around 75 miles per hour. The conditions were so severe that the sails froze, and one barge got fouled on the anchor chain and sank. It was a dangerous spot that tested our seamanship early on.

explicit_text: In 1917, after the U.S. entered World War I, I shipped on the Omsk, a 12,000-ton Russian freighter loaded with cotton, sailing from Baltimore to Liverpool. The crew had mostly deserted due to the Bolshevik upheaval, so we sailed with an American crew and the Russians as passengers. The voyage took us to Halifax and then across the Atlantic under destroyer convoy, facing submarine threats but arriving safely in Liverpool.
implicit_text: In 1917, after the U.S. entered World War I, I shipped on a 12,000-ton Russian freighter loaded with cotton, sailing from a major Mid-Atlantic port to a large British port city. The crew had mostly deserted due to the Bolshevik upheaval, so we sailed with an American crew and the Russians as passengers. The voyage took us to a Canadian port and then across the Atlantic under destroyer convoy, facing submarine threats but arriving safely in the British city.

explicit_text: Baltimore was the port where I boarded the Omsk in 1917. The Russian crew had deserted there, leaving only a few officers aboard, so we had to arrange for an American crew to sail the ship. Baltimore was also near Halifax, where a tragic TNT explosion had recently devastated the harbor.
implicit_text: The Mid-Atlantic port where I boarded the Russian freighter in 1917 had seen the crew desert due to political turmoil, leaving only a few officers aboard, so we had to arrange for an American crew to sail the ship. This port was also near a Canadian harbor where a tragic TNT explosion had recently devastated the area.

explicit_text: Liverpool was our destination in 1917 after crossing the Atlantic on the Omsk under convoy protection. It was a tense voyage with submarine periscopes sighted, but we arrived safely. Later, in early 1918, I was in Liverpool again and had difficulty arranging passage back to the U.S., finally returning on the Adriatic.
implicit_text: The British port city was our destination in 1917 after crossing the Atlantic on the Russian freighter under convoy protection. It was a tense voyage with submarine periscopes sighted, but we arrived safely. Later, in early 1918, I was in that city again and had difficulty arranging passage back to the U.S., finally returning on another ship.

explicit_text: During the upheaval in Russia in 1917, many crew members of the Omsk turned Bolsheviks and deserted the ship in Baltimore. Only four Russians remained aboard, all part owners, while the rest left. This political turmoil directly affected my sailing experience on the Omsk.
implicit_text: During the upheaval in Russia in 1917, many crew members of the Russian freighter turned revolutionaries and deserted the ship in the Mid-Atlantic port. Only four Russians remained aboard, all part owners, while the rest left. This political turmoil directly affected my sailing experience on that vessel.

explicit_text: Halifax was our first stop after leaving Baltimore on the Omsk in 1917. Just days before our arrival, a German agent had caused a massive explosion of a ship loaded with TNT, devastating the harbor. The destruction was severe, with boats hurled onto rocky shores and many casualties, making Halifax a memorable and somber place on that voyage.
implicit_text: The Canadian port was our first stop after leaving the Mid-Atlantic port on the Russian freighter in 1917. Just days before our arrival, a German agent had caused a massive explosion of a ship loaded with TNT, devastating the harbor. The destruction was severe, with boats hurled onto rocky shores and many casualties, making this harbor a memorable and somber place on that voyage.

explicit_text: Germany was the country behind the sabotage in Halifax, where a German agent set off a ship loaded with TNT in 1917. Also, during my Atlantic crossings in World War I, German submarines were a constant threat, with periscopes sighted and torpedoes fired at ships like the Cedric. Germany's naval warfare shaped much of my wartime sailing experience.
implicit_text: The country behind the sabotage in the Canadian harbor, where a German agent set off a ship loaded with TNT in 1917, was also the origin of the submarines that threatened our Atlantic crossings during World War I. Their naval warfare shaped much of my wartime sailing experience.

explicit_text: TNT figured tragically in Halifax in 1917 when a ship loaded with it was exploded by a German agent just before our arrival. The blast caused massive damage to the harbor and killed many people. This event was a stark reminder of the dangers of war even in port cities.
implicit_text: Explosives played a tragic role in the Canadian harbor in 1917 when a ship loaded with them was detonated by a foreign agent just before our arrival. The blast caused massive damage to the harbor and killed many people. This event was a stark reminder of the dangers of war even in port cities.

explicit_text: I crossed the Atlantic Ocean several times during World War I, including voyages on the Omsk and the Adriatic. These crossings were perilous, with submarine threats and convoy escorts by destroyers. The Atlantic was the stage for many tense moments, like when the Cedric was torpedoed and sank nearby.
implicit_text: I crossed the Atlantic Ocean several times during World War I, including voyages on the Russian freighter and another passenger liner. These crossings were perilous, with submarine threats and convoy escorts by warships. The Atlantic was the stage for many tense moments, like when a sister ship was torpedoed and sank nearby.

explicit_text: Destroyers played a crucial role in protecting our convoys during World War I crossings of the Atlantic. They chased submarines, dropped depth charges, and helped rescue survivors after attacks like the sinking of the Cedric. Their presence was vital for our safety on dangerous voyages.
implicit_text: Warships designed for anti-submarine warfare played a crucial role in protecting our convoys during World War I crossings of the Atlantic. They chased submarines, dropped depth charges, and helped rescue survivors after attacks like the sinking of a sister ship. Their presence was vital for our safety on dangerous voyages.

explicit_text: The Cedric was one of our sister ships during the convoy crossing on the Adriatic in early 1918. Tragically, it was torpedoed twice amidships and sank in less than twenty minutes, with about six hundred lives lost. I witnessed the attack from the Adriatic and saw the destroyers fight off the submarines afterward.
implicit_text: One of our sister ships during the convoy crossing on the passenger liner in early 1918 was tragically torpedoed twice amidships and sank in less than twenty minutes, with about six hundred lives lost. I witnessed the attack from the liner and saw the warships fight off the submarines afterward.

explicit_text: After surviving submarine attacks on the Atlantic, we finally reached New York Harbor safely in 1918. It was a relief to arrive without further incident after such dangerous voyages. New York marked the end of that tense transatlantic journey.
implicit_text: After surviving submarine attacks on the Atlantic, we finally reached the major U.S. harbor in 1918 safely. It was a relief to arrive without further incident after such dangerous voyages. That harbor marked the end of that tense transatlantic journey.

explicit_text: I sailed on the Great Lakes later in my career, where sailors had a reputation for superstition about 'Jonah ships' that were considered unlucky. Unlike saltwater sailors, lake sailors were more accepting of women and animals aboard. The Great Lakes presented their own challenges, including pressure from owners on captains to make fast time.
implicit_text: I sailed on the large inland freshwater lakes later in my career, where sailors had a reputation for superstition about 'Jonah ships' that were considered unlucky. Unlike saltwater sailors, lake sailors were more accepting of women and animals aboard. These lakes presented their own challenges, including pressure from owners on captains to make fast time.

explicit_text: Lake Erie was the site of the sinking of the Colgate, a whaleback ship, in 1917. The ship took refuge near Long Point during heavy weather but left too soon due to the captain's inexperience and pressure to make time. The Colgate was heavily loaded and not seaworthy, and the waves caused leaks that sank her before she was out of sight.
implicit_text: One of the Great Lakes was the site of the sinking of a whaleback ship in 1917. The ship took refuge near a prominent peninsula during heavy weather but left too soon due to the captain's inexperience and pressure to make time. The ship was heavily loaded and not seaworthy, and the waves caused leaks that sank her before she was out of sight.

explicit_text: Long Point, Ontario was where the Colgate sought shelter during a gale on Lake Erie in 1917. Many ships waited out the storm there, but the Colgate's captain decided to leave early and was lost at sea. Long Point was a critical landmark for lake sailors during storms.
implicit_text: A prominent peninsula in Ontario was where the whaleback ship sought shelter during a gale on one of the Great Lakes in 1917. Many ships waited out the storm there, but the ship's captain decided to leave early and was lost at sea. That peninsula was a critical landmark for lake sailors during storms.

explicit_text: The Colgate was a whaleback ship that sank in 1917 on Lake Erie near Long Point. The captain, inexperienced and pressured to keep schedule, left shelter during a gale, and the ship took on leaks and sank. This tragedy illustrated the dangers of greed and poor judgment on the lakes.
implicit_text: The whaleback ship that sank in 1917 on one of the Great Lakes near a prominent peninsula was lost due to the captain's inexperience and pressure to keep schedule. Leaving shelter during a gale, the ship took on leaks and sank. This tragedy illustrated the dangers of greed and poor judgment on the lakes.

explicit_text: During Prohibition, I sailed on a private yacht owned by a Chicago manufacturer. After leaving Detroit, the owner surprisingly opened his liquor closet to the crew, which was quite a treat given the scarcity of alcohol. When we reached Chicago, the crew was arrested for violating Prohibition laws, but the owner played innocent and we were released after a search.
implicit_text: During Prohibition, I sailed on a private yacht owned by a manufacturer from a major Midwestern city. After leaving a nearby industrial city, the owner surprisingly opened his liquor closet to the crew, which was quite a treat given the scarcity of alcohol. When we reached that Midwestern city, the crew was arrested for violating Prohibition laws, but the owner played innocent and we were released after a search.

explicit_text: Detroit was the port we left from on the private yacht during Prohibition. The owner shocked us by offering his liquor to the crew, which was unusual and generous. This voyage ended with an arrest in Chicago but was memorable for the rare chance to drink freely.
implicit_text: We left from an industrial city in the Midwest on the private yacht during Prohibition. The owner shocked us by offering his liquor to the crew, which was unusual and generous. This voyage ended with an arrest in a major Midwestern city but was memorable for the rare chance to drink freely.

explicit_text: During Prohibition, I sailed on a private yacht where the owner secretly gave the crew access to his liquor stash, which was a rare and joyous occasion. Despite our drinking, we were arrested in Chicago for violating the laws, but the owner managed to avoid trouble by playing innocent. This experience showed the contradictions and challenges sailors faced during Prohibition.
